Ad Text:
Mirvac is a brand that creates and curates better experiences.

We are more than developers or builders - we are visionaries, and our ability to see the world differently drives us to be bold, embrace innovation and diversity, and lead with optimism. As creators of positive change, we see each new project as an opportunity to leave a lasting legacy, delivering enduring value for all Australians.

We focus on sustainability, innovation, and shared value, to return benefits to our customers, partners, investors, and communities. Join us as we work towards building the imagine nation.

Your Opportunity

This is a permanent opportunity to play a pivotal role in Mirvac's AI & Automation platform - core to our integrated digital operating model. You'll lead the end‑to‑end design and delivery of intelligent, agent‑oriented automation while shaping the platform roadmap and uplifting engineering standards across the business. You'll be supported by a collaborative, high‑performing team and a manager who champions innovation, trust, and continuous growth.

Your Responsibilities

Partner closely with business stakeholders to understand pain points, processes, and opportunities; craft and document AI and data use cases for inclusion in platform backlogs and prioritization discussions.
Architect and lead delivery of complex, orchestrated solutions that coordinate AI models, bots, APIs, data services, and human oversight-optimising for security, scalability, and performance.
Define and apply agentic design patterns, including confidence thresholds, escalation paths, fallback mechanisms, and recovery strategies to ensure safe and reliable automation.
Engage in forward-deployed engagements, embedding with business teams to co-design and prototype Agentic capabilities that deliver measurable value and rapid feedback loops.
Establish and enforce engineering, testing, and documentation standards, including consideration of AI failure modes, exception handling, and auditability.
Design and maintain human-in-the-loop controls, ensuring appropriate levels of autonomy, transparency, and accountability in AI-enabled processes.
Act as the primary point of contact for managed services and vendors, overseeing onboarding, delivery, ongoing management, and ensuring strong platform partnerships and vendor performance.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams including product managers, data architects, and technology partners to identify and deliver high-impact opportunities aligned with strategic platform roadmaps.
Ensure compliance with enterprise security, privacy, and risk policies across all AI and Automation initiatives.
Define, track, and report metrics

Your Point of Difference

The ideal candidate will hold relevant tertiary qualifications in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, or a closely related discipline, providing a strong theoretical foundation to support complex technical and architectural decision‑making.

You will also bring:

7+ years in technology roles with extensive hands-on enterprise automation experience with demonstrated technical delivery expertise (e.g. UiPath)
1-3 years' experience in using and incorporating AI/ML into workflows.
Experience with modern orchestration and agent frameworks on Azure/AWS for deploying Agentic AI solutions.
Experience building and scaling conversational AI experiences.
Proven ability to collaborate with and manage vendors and managed service providers effectively.
Solid understanding of enterprise data platforms and ability to work with data warehouses (e.g., Snowflake).

Apply today!

If you're excited by the opportunity to shape the future of AI and automation while creating lasting impact, we'd love to hear from you. Apply now to join Mirvac and be part of a team building smarter, more sustainable experiences for generations to come.

Lead AI Engineer

Deliver AI-powered solutions that transform the experience for employees and for millions of members.
Permanent, full-time position
Flexible, hybrid working environment

At AustralianSuper, we truly care about our colleagues. We know work and life are intertwined. That's why we support the diverse needs of everyone and have policies that enable us all to thrive and be truly flexible. We ensure diversity is celebrated for the opportunity it provides us all to learn and grow and deliver better outcomes for members.

Your New Role

This role is about moving GenAI from proof‑of‑concept to trusted, enterprise‑grade systems used every day - safely, responsibly, and at scale.

As our Lead AI Engineer (internally known as Lead GenAI Systems Engineer), you'll design, build, and deliver production‑grade AI solutions that move beyond experimentation and into real‑world impact. You'll lead the technical design and delivery of GenAI solutions, from LLM and RAG foundations through to agentic AI systems that orchestrate tools, workflows, and decision‑making in production.

This is a builder‑leader role. You'll provide technical leadership for a small, high‑impact team of AI engineers, while remaining deeply hands‑on in solution design, development, and delivery. You'll have end‑to‑end ownership from concept and prototyping through to production deployment, adoption, and ongoing improvement.

You'll work closely with product, data, technology, and risk partners to ensure solutions are robust, scalable, secure, and responsibly designed, aligned with enterprise standards and regulatory expectations.

If you enjoy working at the edge of what's possible with AI, this role offers both challenge and impact.

What You'll Need



You don't need to tick every box, but you'll bring strong experience across many of the following areas:

Strong hands‑on experience building GenAI solutions, including LLM integration, prompt engineering, RAG patterns, and evaluation approaches in production environments.
Demonstrated capability in prompt design and optimisation, including structuring system and user prompts, managing context, reducing hallucinations, and improving output consistency and reliability.
Experience designing and delivering agentic AI systems, such as tool‑using agents, workflow orchestration, multi‑step reasoning, and human‑in‑the‑loop patterns.
Hands‑on expertise with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP), including deploying and operating AI solutions in cloud‑based environments.
Experience with containerisation and orchestration, including Docker and Kubernetes, to support scalable, reliable AI services.
Advanced programming skills in Python and/or JavaScript, used to build, integrate, and operate GenAI and agentic systems across APIs, services, and workflows.
Demonstrated experience with MLOps / GenAI operations practices, including lifecycle management, deployment pipelines, monitoring, and operational support.
Experience embedding and operationalising Responsible AI and ethical AI principles, applying best‑practice patterns across GenAI and agentic use cases in enterprise or regulated environments.
Strong software engineering and system design skills, with the ability to balance experimentation with production readiness, performance, cost, and maintainability.
Experience leading technical delivery, providing clear technical direction, reviewing designs and code, and supporting the development of other engineers while remaining hands‑on.
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills, able to translate complex technical concepts for both technical and non‑technical audiences.
An innovative, curious, and growth‑oriented mindset, with a desire to continuously learn and apply emerging AI capabilities in practical ways.
Relevant certifications and experience in financial services or large global organisations are highly regarded.

Life at AustralianSuper


AustralianSuper is committed to colleague development, and we support our people with ongoing learning, coaching and training, as well as career opportunities across our expanding global organisation. We offer generous leave entitlements and promote a blended working environment in which all roles can flex, and we're happy to discuss what this looks like for you.

We cultivate a workplace that champions safety, respect, inclusiveness and diversity. We are committed to supporting our diverse workforce in a way that is inclusive and embraces diversity in all its forms. If you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process or the role, please let our recruitment team know.

What's Next


If you're ready to bring your energy, integrity, generosity of spirit and passion for excellent outcomes to a team that loves a challenge, apply now!

Australian or New Zealand citizenship or Australian permanent residency status is required.

Progress powered by purpose.

AustralianSuper may use AI to review resumes and conduct initial phone interviews. This involves processing your resume, cover letter, and voice recording in Australia and the USA to help assess your suitability for the applied role. When invited to an AI phone screening, you may opt for a traditional phone screening instead. For information on how we handle your personal data, please refer to our Candidate Privacy Policy.

https://www.australiansuper.com/careers/candidate-privacy-notice

Agencies please note: this vacancy is being managed directly by AustralianSuper's Talent Acquisition team. We will contact our preferred agency partners should we require additional support. Thank you.

Senior Software Engineer (AI)

📍 Sydney, Australia
💰 Up to $210,000 package (inc Super) + Bonus
🕒 Permanent | Mid-level to Mid-Senior


About

Driven by our AI-first strategy, we are creating AI-native solutions and an AI-empowered workforce to deliver best-in-class experiences for over 2 million Australians. You will join our talented Enterprise AI Enablement team to build leading-edge solutions within our agentic AI ecosystem.

What Role Will You Play?

As a Software Engineer within the Enterprise AI Enablement team, you will be at the forefront of designing and building robust, scalable backend systems that power our AI-native solutions.

With a strong emphasis on software engineering excellence, you will spend the majority of your time writing production-grade code and automating complex workflows to create a resilient foundation for our agentic AI ecosystem.

You will:


Take end-to-end ownership of products in a modern DevOps culture
Design, architect, build, test, and operate scalable backend systems
Work alongside data scientists in a multidisciplinary AI team
Implement LLMOps best practices for deploying and monitoring AI models
Build reliable, observable, and production-ready AI platforms

This role is ideal for a software engineer who has transitioned into Data/ML & AI and wants to build enterprise-grade AI systems at scale.

What You Offer


5+ years' experience as a Software Engineer in an agile DevOps environment
Strong object-oriented programming experience (Python preferred)
Experience across the full SDLC: design, build, test, and support mission-critical production platforms
Proven ability to write robust, production-grade code and automate complex workflows
Hands-on experience with CI/CD pipelines
Experience managing deployments in containerised cloud environments (Docker/Kubernetes)
Exposure to modern agentic AI frameworks and concepts such as:
RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
MCP
GCP Vertex AI
Google ADK
LangChain / LangGraph
LlamaIndex

If you feel you possess the relevant skills to the above, apply now with most updated CV!

About nxzen

nxzen is a global technology, consulting, and engineering partner supporting the energy, utilities, and national infrastructure sectors. We help organisations optimise performance, digitise operations, and build safer, smarter, and more resilient systems by combining deep domain expertise with innovative technology.

Our work spans the full infrastructure lifecycle, partnering with clients to deliver practical, outcome-driven solutions across operations, transformation, digital platforms, and engineering. Through our specialist teams and Centres of Excellence, we support the delivery of future-ready infrastructure that enhances efficiency, resilience, and long-term sustainability.

About the ROLE: Head of Data & AI

Location: Sydney

Permanent Full-time Role

The Head of Data & AI (Artificial Intelligence) will shape, build, and enable the delivery of nxzen’s Data and Artificial Intelligence proposition, with a primary focus on leveraging AI for Asset Intelligence and Operational Excellence. The role targets Utilities in Australia generally, with a particular emphasis on Australian Electricity Distribution Networks.

This role reports to the Digital CoE (Centre of Excellence) Head and collaborates closely with colleagues in the wider nxzen group. Including but not limited to other CoE teams, Client Partners, other group companies, etc. The role is both market-facing and works internally to build our capabilities in applying Artificial Intelligence to drive smarter asset management, predictive analytics, Operational Excellence, and performance improvement across utilities in Australia, with a particular focus on electricity distribution networks.

Key responsibilities for the role include: 

Strategy & Growth

Define and execute the Data & AI (Artificial Intelligence) strategy for Asset Intelligence and Operational Excellence, aligned to nxzen's business objectives and the needs of Australian utility clients.

Identify and prioritise opportunities to expand nxzen's footprint across Australian Electricity Distribution Networks and the broader utilities sector.

Work with Client Partners, clients, and NNNCo to develop go-to-market solutions and bids that combine AI-driven analytics, IoT, and asset performance improvement.

Develop compelling value propositions that demonstrate how Artificial Intelligence can enhance asset intelligence, operational efficiency, and network resilience for electricity distribution businesses.

Client & Stakeholder Engagement

Act as a senior point of contact for key utility clients, particularly Australian Electricity Distribution Networks, building trusted relationships with operations, digital, and engineering leaders.

Support Client Partners and Delivery Directors in shaping client transformation journeys that leverage AI for asset intelligence and operational excellence outcomes.

Represent nxzen externally at industry events, contributing to thought leadership and brand positioning in the Australian utilities and energy sector.

Service & Solution Development

Shape the Data & AI product and service roadmap, ensuring solutions are technically robust, scalable, and commercially competitive for the Australian electricity distribution market.

Drive the development of AI-powered solutions for asset health monitoring, predictive maintenance, risk assessment, network optimisation, and operational decision-making.

Maintain awareness of emerging technologies and trends (AI/ML, generative AI, IoT, 5G, edge computing, OT security, digital twins) and translate these into practical solutions for utility clients.

Programme Delivery Oversight

Oversee successful delivery of Data & AI programmes for utility clients, ensuring outcomes are met on quality, cost, and schedule.

Implement strong governance, assurance, and performance metrics across projects, with particular attention to the regulatory and compliance environment of Australian electricity distribution.

Ensure operational compliance with security, safety, and regulatory requirements applicable to Australian utility networks.

About YOU

Essential Knowledge

Extensive experience in Data & Artificial Intelligence, with a demonstrated ability to apply AI/ML techniques to asset intelligence and operational excellence in utility or infrastructure environments.

Deep understanding of Australian Electricity Distribution Networks, including regulatory frameworks (AER), network management challenges, and asset lifecycle management.

Proven ability to operate across IT, OT, and ET environments, integrating diverse systems to drive digital transformation and enhance asset performance using AI.

Hands-on experience managing, analysing, and deriving insights from IoT devices, OT systems, digital twins, simulation platforms, and asset health models (e.g., NARMS, CNAIM).

Demonstrated track record of leveraging AI and integrated data to inform asset performance improvement, risk assessment, and strategic decision-making.

Extensive experience applying IEC CIM, ISO 14224, and Ops 4.0 frameworks to support AI-driven asset intelligence and operational excellence initiatives.

Skilled in collaborating with cross-functional teams and stakeholders to design and deliver technically robust, scalable, and commercially viable AI-powered asset intelligence solutions.

Bachelor’s degree in engineering, Computer Science, Data Science, Information Technology, or a related discipline.

Desirable Experience

Experience selling or delivering AI and data solutions to Australian electricity distribution businesses (e.g., Ausgrid, Endeavour Energy, Essential Energy, AusNet, Powercor, SA Power Networks, Energex, Ergon Energy, Western Power, or similar).

Familiarity with Australian energy market structures, the National Electricity Rules, and the regulatory investment test (RIT-D) processes.

Experience with AI/ML platforms and tools commonly used in utility asset management and operational analytics.

Track record of building and leading high-performing teams in a consulting or professional services environment.

Professional certifications in Data & AI, IoT, or Analytics (e.g., Certified Data Scientist, AI/ML certifications, or Analytics Professional certifications).

ISO 55000 / Asset Management certification.

IEC CIM or related utility data modelling standards.

OT / Cybersecurity certifications (e.g., CISSP, CISA, or relevant OT security training).

 How to APPLY

To apply, please submit a copy of your updated resume. Screening will be conducted as we receive applications, and ONLY shortlisted applicants will be contacted.

For more information or to have a confidential discussion, please contact our People & Culture Manager, Shreya Pahwa on shreya.pahwa@nxzen.com.

Job Title: Senior / Staff Software Engineer (AI Systems & Platforms)
Ad Text:
Senior / Staff Software Engineer (AI Systems & Platforms)

Location: Sydney CBD

Salary: Up to $250,000 + super + ESOP

About the Company

We’re working with a newly formed, AI native holding company that is redefining how services businesses are acquired, transformed, and scaled across Australia and New Zealand.

Backed by venture funding with further investment on the horizon, the business is in its earliest stage, currently a tight knit team of three founders, with significant momentum and an ambitious roadmap ahead.

With demand already outpacing engineering capacity, they are now building out their founding engineering team.

The Opportunity:

This is a rare chance to join at the ground floor and help build core platforms from scratch.

You’ll be working directly with the founders to design and engineer complex, scalable systems that underpin multiple businesses. The work is deeply technical and suited to engineers who enjoy solving hard problems, thinking from first principles, and operating with a high degree of autonomy.

Expect a highly experimental environment where building, testing, and iterating is part of the daily rhythm.

What You’ll Be Doing:

Designing and building core platform infrastructure from day one

Working on complex system architecture and distributed systems

Developing AI driven systems, including agentic workflows and RAG pipelines

Contributing to research, experimentation, and rapid prototyping

Collaborating closely with founders on technical direction and strategy

What They’re Looking For:

Strong engineering fundamentals with first principles thinking

Experience designing and building complex, scalable systems

Practical experience working with AI/ML systems

Exposure to areas such as:

Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)

Knowledge representation

Agent based or autonomous systems

Proactive mindset, with side projects or self driven work highly regarded

Comfortable working in an early stage, high ownership environment

Tech Environment:

Primary: TypeScript

Also in use: Python, Rust

Exploring: Go

Note: Experience with this exact stack is not required.

Why Join?

Build from zero, genuine greenfield engineering

Work directly with experienced, highly technical founders

Equity upside through ESOP

High impact role with real ownership from day one

Opportunity to shape both systems and engineering culture

Working Model:

5 days per week in office (Sydney CBD)

Interview Process:

3 stage process

All interviews are face to face

Approx. 1 hour per interview

Apply now for full info

Ad Text:
Can you see yourself taking the next step in your professional engineering career as a Mechatronics / Automation Engineer specialising in electrical, automation and control system design for our clients global manufacturing customers? Our client has a proud history of leading innovation in this space and is looking for a like-minded professional to join their business. 

Your role

The design, installation, supervision, programming, testing, commissioning and fault finding of electrical, automation and control systems
To provide technical support and solutions across our client's product range, both internally and to our client's customers
To work with our client's internal sales team and their customers to define scope of works and deliver cost-effective solutions up front for projects


About you

A relevant bachelor level engineering degree, most likely in Mechatronics Engineering
Around 5 to 10 years post graduate proven experience in design, programming and commissioning of electrical automation and control systems 
PLC Programing and configuration on Beckhoff PLCs and motion systems (TwinCAT 2/3) mandatory.
Experience in the development and maintenance of control software in C# and Python
Ability to do irregular travel nationally and globally in support of commissioning at our client's customers
Well-developed problem solving, interpersonal and communication skills
This is a genuine opportunity for career development in a fast paced, flexible, autonomous environment where innovation is highly valued. So, if your values align with those of our client: Innovation and Excellence; a Customer-Centric Approach; Collaboration and Teamwork; and Global Reach, Local Touch, this could be the role for you.

We request that candidates have full Australian working rights

Please submit your resume in WORD format ONLY.

Company Description


Lendi Group is a market-leading digital platform business that is focused on revolutionising the entire property journey. It operates Lendi - Australia’s original and #1 digital mortgage brand - and the iconic Aussie franchise.

Powered by an advanced AI proprietary platform, a national network of 1,300 brokers, 230 retail stores, a team of experienced buyer’s agents, integrated property search and conveyancing, Aussie is supporting customers at every step of their property journey to find, buy and own with more confidence than ever before. Lendi Group’s loan book is more than $100b and it was named Australia’s Aggregator of the Year for 2024.

To learn more about life at Lendi Group check out our blog: https://www.lendi.com.au/inspire/category/life-at-lendi/

Innovation at Our Core – We challenge the status quo and push boundaries to create better solutions, with a goal of becoming AI-native by June 2026.
Work with the Best – Collaborate with some of the brightest minds in fintech, financial services, and strategy.
Make an Impact – Contribute to meaningful projects that shape our business and the future of property finance.
Grow & Evolve – Develop your skills and advance your career in a fast-moving, purpose-driven environment.

About the role:

The Platform Security Engineer is a valued member of our engineering team. We value evidence-backed opinions and offer the opportunity to play an integral part in shaping engineering rhythm, culture, and processes across planning, architecture and design, coding, deployment, and support.


In an ever-changing environment, Lendi Group is leading the charge in developing industry-first technology designed to offer Australian homeowners transparency, simplicity, and convenience in their home loan experience.
The Platform Security Engineer partners with engineering teams to evaluate planned changes, minimise security risk, and continuously improve our security posture. A key focus of the role is driving vulnerability and security findings down over time, improving security standards, and embedding secure-by-default practices across both traditional and AI-enabled systems.

Please note: this role will be a 12 Month Fixed Term Contract

Job Description


Working closely with the Cyber Security team to ensure security principles are consistently applied across the engineering environment.
Collaborating and coding with engineering teams to uplift secure development practices and reduce recurring security findings.
Designing, building, and maintaining secure cloud and microservice solutions.
Improving security posture through effective use of COTS products, platform controls, and network infrastructure.
Vulnerability and Threat Management — monitoring and assessing assets including cloud infrastructure, containers, packages, APIs, and requests, with a focus on remediation and measurable risk reduction.
Supporting the secure design and operation of AI-enabled systems, including LLM integrations, automation agents, and MCP-based architectures.
Implementing and reviewing AI security guardrails such as access controls, data protection, prompt-injection mitigation, and output validation.
Collaborating with cross-functional teams including engineering, operations, compliance, data, and AI platform teams.
Contributing as an individual contributor within a cross-functional DevOps / SecOps team.
Build or integrate services to improve security and compliance

Qualifications


The ideal candidate is passionate about promoting a security-first culture and has a strong, current understanding of security best practices, with particular focus on cloud security, secure design, incident handling, and operational resilience.
Experience securing microservices in cloud environments and working with configuration-as-code is important for this role. Experience securing AI-driven systems, including LLM-powered services and internal automation, is highly regarded.
Strong communication skills are essential. We value engineers who can clearly communicate risk, trade-offs, and remediation paths, and who approach collaboration with empathy for engineering challenges.

Key Skills

Strong understanding of networking, infrastructure, and applications from a DevOps perspective with a security focus.
Hands-on experience implementing and maintaining security controls across cloud environments (particularly AWS) and access management solutions (e.g. Auth0).
Experience with security logging, monitoring, and vulnerability assessment, with a demonstrated ability to drive findings down and improve baseline security standards.
Passion for secure coding practices and identifying common implementation and operational security risks.

Desirable Experience

AWS technologies such as CloudFormation, Terraform, IAM, WAF, Node.js and/or TypeScript.
Docker, containerised environments, serverless architectures, and Kubernetes.
Event-driven systems.
Securing AI platforms, including LLM integrations, MCP implementations, AI guardrails, and automation/build agents.

Additional Information


Lendi Group teams operate across Australia and the Philippines. Bringing together diverse expertise, innovative technology and a customer-first approach, our teams work seamlessly to simplify the property journey, helping Australians find, buy and own property with confidence.

We support our people in a variety of ways, but a few of the benefits that our people rave about include:

A vibrant, relaxed, yet professional culture.
Hybrid working arrangement designed to support work-life balance, while fostering meaningful connection and collaboration.
A holistic wellbeing programs offering 24/7 support, including medical, mental health, and financial wellbeing services to enable our workforce to thrive at home and work.
Generous paid Parental Leave: we celebrate our growing Lendi Group family with 18-26 weeks leave for primary carers and up to 4 weeks for secondary carers.
An additional week’s Loyalty Leave each year after reaching 3 years’ service.
Wellness initiatives with a strong focus on psychological safety.

We’re committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive community at Lendi Group. We believe that a team reflecting the world around us leads to greater innovation, stronger collaboration, and a more engaging workplace.

Our culture is guided by our 3 core values: We Are Stronger United; Act Like You Own It; and Keep Home Loans Human. Our values are part of our core DNA that helps Lendi Group to attract, engage and evolve the right talent and build best-in-class products.

This is an opportunity to shape the future of a fast-growing, purpose-driven company that’s transforming the homeownership journey.

Ready to contribute to Lendi Group’s next chapter? Apply now and be part of something big!

Job Title: Mid Level Software Engineer - AI Native Fintech
Ad Text:
Company Description


Lendi Group is a market-leading digital platform business that is focused on revolutionising the entire property journey. It operates Lendi - Australia’s original and #1 digital mortgage brand - and the iconic Aussie franchise.

Powered by an advanced AI proprietary platform, a national network of 1,300 brokers, 230 retail stores, a team of experienced buyer’s agents, integrated property search and conveyancing, Aussie is supporting customers at every step of their property journey to find, buy and own with more confidence than ever before. Lendi Group’s loan book is more than $100b and it was named Australia’s Aggregator of the Year for 2024.

To learn more about life at Lendi Group check out our blog: https://www.lendi.com.au/inspire/category/life-at-lendi/

Innovation at Our Core – We challenge the status quo and push boundaries to create better solutions, with a goal of becoming AI-native by June 2026.
Work with the Best – Collaborate with some of the brightest minds in fintech, financial services, and strategy.
Make an Impact – Contribute to meaningful projects that shape our business and the future of property finance.
Grow & Evolve – Develop your skills and advance your career in a fast-moving, purpose-driven environment.

About the role

Build the future of fintech — with AI at the core
Lendi Group is evolving into an AI-native fintech, embedding artificial intelligence into how we design, build, and operate our products. This isn’t about bolting on tools — it’s about empowering engineers to solve harder problems, move faster, and deliver smarter, more personalised experiences for millions of Australians.
We’re investing heavily in upskilling, experimentation, and modern engineering practices, giving our teams access to cutting-edge AI capabilities and the autonomy to shape how they’re used. If you’re curious, collaborative, and excited by what AI can unlock in real-world products — this is your moment.


We’re looking for a Mid Level Software Engineer to join our established team with high amount of BAU — You’ll work alongside passionate, experienced engineers tackling complex product and platform challenges, while helping define how AI is practically applied across our engineering lifecycle. You’ll have genuine influence — on architecture, engineering standards, delivery rhythm, and the evolution of an AI-enabled fintech platform — all in a team that values learning, ownership, and having fun while doing meaningful work.

Job Description


Own and deliver robust, scalable systems, influencing architecture and design decisions
Practise a true “you build it, you own it” mindset — from development to monitoring and support
Build clean, maintainable code with strong CI/CD practices and modern engineering standards

Work as a full-stack engineer, building backend services in Node.js (TypeScript) with React front-ends
Collaborate closely with Product Managers, Designers, BAs, and Engineers in cross-functional agile squads
Partner with Product and UX to shape intuitive customer journeys and interaction patterns
Drive team rhythm across planning, delivery, deployment, and continuous improvement
Mentor and uplift engineers across the team and broader engineering group
Apply AI tools thoughtfully across the SDLC to improve productivity, quality, and outcomes

Qualifications


Core experience
5+ years of backend development experience
Strong proficiency in Node.js (TypeScript) and modern backend ecosystems
Strong API design skills: REST, schema-first design, GraphQL, webhooks, and auth (Auth0, JWT)
Deep understanding of microservices architectures and messaging patterns (event-driven, pub/sub, request-response)

Hands-on experience with Kafka, Docker, and Kubernetes
Strong AWS experience (IAM, Lambda, DynamoDB, ECS, SSM)
Experience with DevOps and Infrastructure-as-Code approaches

AI & modern engineering (new and evolving)

Strong understanding of AI tools and their practical use across the SDLC
Experience or exposure to AI engineering, including:
Building Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines
Prompt engineering for effective LLM use cases
Working with LLM frameworks such as LangChain, Semantic Kernel, Agno, or similar
Exposure to agentic AI (AI agents, task orchestration, automation workflows)
Willingness to work across a variety of AI tools as the ecosystem evolves

Nice to have (but not essential)

Experience with Kotlin (used in parts of our Partner Product space)
Exposure to MongoDB, including schema design, indexing, and change streams
Experience streaming or integrating data into platforms like Databricks
Analytics and observability tools such as Amplitude and Datadog
Experience with Fastify (or similar Node frameworks) and Zod schema validation
Knowledge of Auth0 + OpenFGA and multi-client authentication flows
Understanding of security and compliance in an AI-enabled environment

Why Lendi Group?
Build real products used by millions, not experiments in isolation

Be part of a genuine AI-native transformation, not just “AI theatre”
Learn, experiment, and lead with modern AI and cloud technologies
Work with smart, supportive engineers who value ownership and craft
Shape the future of fintech while continuing to grow your own skills

If you’re excited by AI-enabled engineering, ownership at scale, and building meaningful fintech products — we’d love to hear from you.

Additional Information


Lendi Group teams operate across Australia and the Philippines. Bringing together diverse expertise, innovative technology and a customer-first approach, our teams work seamlessly to simplify the property journey, helping Australians find, buy and own property with confidence.

We support our people in a variety of ways, but a few of the benefits that our people rave about include:

A vibrant, relaxed, yet professional culture.

Company Description


Lendi Group is a market-leading digital platform business that is focused on revolutionising the entire property journey. It operates Lendi - Australia’s original and #1 digital mortgage brand - and the iconic Aussie franchise.

Powered by an advanced AI proprietary platform, a national network of 1,300 brokers, 230 retail stores, a team of experienced buyer’s agents, integrated property search and conveyancing, Aussie is supporting customers at every step of their property journey to find, buy and own with more confidence than ever before. Lendi Group’s loan book is more than $100b and it was named Australia’s Aggregator of the Year for 2024.

To learn more about life at Lendi Group check out our blog: https://www.lendi.com.au/inspire/category/life-at-lendi/

Why Join Us?

Innovation at Our Core – We challenge the status quo and push boundaries to create better solutions.
Work with the Best – Collaborate with some of the brightest minds in fintech, financial services, and strategy.
Make an Impact – Contribute to meaningful projects that shape our business and the future of property finance.
Grow & Evolve – Develop your skills and advance your career in a fast-moving, purpose-driven environment.

As a Lead Data Scientist, you'll lead a growing data science team and play a pivotal role in shaping and delivering data-driven solutions across stakeholders. Your primary responsibilities include leading and mentoring your team, designing and implementing advanced machine learning and statistical models, translating complex business problems into scalable data products, and leading the end-to-end lifecycle from discovery to production. You will lead by example with modern data science techniques and tooling, providing clear technical direction and coaching to team members. 

We’re looking for a collaborative team player who thrives in a dynamic environment, embraces the challenge of legacy systems, remains composed under pressure, and is driven to deliver continuous improvement and strategic impact.  This role provides a unique opportunity to connect directly with our stakeholders.  You should be a versatile data leader who can both drive hands-on delivery and lead the development of new data initiatives—balancing strategic oversight with practical execution. 

Job Description


On a typical day you will: 

Lead, mentor and motivate a data science team (up to10 people) and set the technical direction and standards 
Collaborate within a cross-functional digital team, including Product Management, Engineering and Design. 
Bridge the gap between business problems and data science solutions, turning opportunities into impactful, production-ready models. 
Utilise strong analytical and modelling skills to design, build, and validate machine learning solutions, particularly tree-based methods (e.g. XGBoost, GBM, LightGBM), neural networks, and transformer-based approaches. 
Define and lead the end-to-end lifecycle of data products in an agile environment, from problem framing and experimentation through to deployment and monitoring. 
Conduct and document detailed analysis, including business case development, to prioritise use cases and clearly communicate trade-offs and expected value to stakeholders. 
Work closely with data and platform engineers on ETL pipelines, containerised solutions (e.g. Docker, Kubernetes), and cloud-based environments (e.g. GCP, AWS, Azure), with exposure to modern data platforms such as Snowflake and Databricks. 
Collaborate with cross-functional Agile teams (Engineering, Product, Design, QA) in remote and on-site settings, applying appropriate governance and engagement models to data projects. 
Provide BAU support for existing models and data products, while actively managing demand, prioritisation, and stakeholder expectations to create space for strategic, high-impact data science initiatives. 

Qualifications


Proven end-to-end Data Science experience (6+ years), including recent experience operating at Senior/Lead level 
Proven ability to lead, mentor and motivate a data science team (typically 3–10 people) and to set technical direction and standards 
Strong, hands-on expertise with modern machine learning models and algorithms, particularly tree-based methods (e.g. XGBoost, GBM, LightGBM), statistical models, and neural networks 
Demonstrated experience working with LLMs, transformers and embeddings in practical, production-oriented use cases 
Solid understanding of the end-to-end lifecycle of data products, from problem framing and model development through to productisation, pipelining and BAU support 
Proficiency in Python and a strong command of SQL for data exploration, feature engineering and model implementation 
Experience working with cloud platforms (e.g. GCP, AWS or Azure) and familiarity with modern data ecosystems such as Snowflake and/or Databricks (or ability to upskill quickly) 
Exposure to containerised solutions (e.g. Docker) and collaborating with engineers on production deployment and MLOps practices 
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to translate business problems into data solutions and tell a compelling story with insights 
Experience contributing to or leading governance and engagement models (e.g. steering committees, RACI/RASCI frameworks, working groups) across multiple stakeholders 
Demonstrated capability in prioritisation and managing tradeoffs in a resourceconstrained or scaleup environment, including pushing back on lowvalue work 
Experience developing or contributing to business cases for data and AI initiatives, including articulating value, risks and assumptions
